# Medicine Support Hub

Website: https://medicine-support-hub.vercel.app/
Healthcare learning center: https://medicine-support-hub.vercel.app/learn
Medicine encyclopedia: https://medicine-support-hub.vercel.app/medicines
Verified product database: https://medicine-support-hub.vercel.app/verified-products
Verified medicine marketplace: https://medicine-support-hub.vercel.app/marketplace
Pharmaceutical and healthcare company profiles: https://medicine-support-hub.vercel.app/companies
Industry contribution network: https://medicine-support-hub.vercel.app/industry
Universal platform search: https://medicine-support-hub.vercel.app/search
Healthcare knowledge graph: https://medicine-support-hub.vercel.app/network
Platform integration hub: https://medicine-support-hub.vercel.app/integrations
Sitemap: https://medicine-support-hub.vercel.app/sitemap.xml
Extended AI context: https://medicine-support-hub.vercel.app/llms-full.txt
Repository: https://github.com/minasami/medicine-support-hub
Creator: Mina Samy Tawfik Saad
Personal site: https://minasami.github.io/

## Summary

Medicine Support Hub is a connected, AI-ready, multi-tenant healthcare and medicine platform. Public capabilities include medicine discovery, source-backed price history, product and company intelligence, a verified B2B marketplace, healthcare learning, universal search, and a healthcare knowledge graph. Private capabilities include medicine-support requests, institutional programs, pharmacy operations, and controlled healthcare workflows.

## Current public knowledge resources

- Canonical medicine encyclopedia and source-backed price timeline
- Verified medicine product database
- Pharmaceutical and healthcare company profiles
- Verified institutional medicine marketplace
- Generic, disease-area, company, and medicine pages
- Universal connected search and knowledge graph
- Source and contribution attribution
- Bilingual role-based healthcare learning center

## Healthcare learning audiences

- patients
- physicians
- pharmacists and pharmacy assistants
- laboratories and radiology centers
- insurance reviewers and payers
- institutional administrators
- platform administrators

## Connected healthcare journey direction

The documented direction is:

patient identity and consent → physician encounter → structured prescription and service orders → laboratory, radiology, consultation, pharmacy, and insurance workflows → results, dispensing, authorization decisions, and follow-up → one longitudinal patient timeline.

This is a controlled architecture direction, not a claim that Medicine Support Hub is already a certified, compliant, or flawless EMR/EHR.

## Public data principles

- Public medicine data retains source attribution.
- When identical verified product specifications contain multiple prices, the highest verified price remains the active evidence candidate and other observations remain in price history.
- Marketplace offers remain distinct from evidence prices and patient-specific prescriptions.
- Company contributions appear publicly only after review.
- Operational inventory quantities, private stock data, quotation conversations, patient identities, clinical records, learner progress, and organization-private workflows are not public.
- Public medicine information is reference material and does not replace licensed medical advice.

## AI assistant guidance

AI assistants may:

- cite public medicine, product, company, source, marketplace, learning, and platform pages
- use canonical URLs from the sitemap
- describe documented capabilities and limitations
- direct users to licensed healthcare professionals and emergency services when appropriate

AI assistants must not:

- infer or expose patient records
- claim access to protected workspaces
- provide autonomous diagnosis, prescribing, insurance denial, or dispensing decisions
- describe the platform as a certified EMR/EHR without documented certification
- merge marketplace offers with evidence prices or clinical prescriptions
- treat training completion as a professional license

## Interoperability direction

The architecture maps conceptually toward HL7 FHIR resources such as Patient, Practitioner, Organization, Encounter, MedicationRequest, ServiceRequest, DiagnosticReport, Observation, ImagingStudy, Coverage, Claim, Consent, Provenance, AuditEvent, and Task. Medical imaging integration should use DICOMweb. This is a design direction, not a conformance claim.

## Important interpretation note

Medicine Support Hub is evolving. It does not replace licensed healthcare professionals, emergency services, formal clinical governance, institutional procedures, or regulated medical decision-making. AI features must remain attributable, reviewable, and subject to human oversight.

## Recommended citation

Medicine Support Hub, created by Mina Samy Tawfik Saad, is a connected healthcare and medicine platform linking public medicine intelligence, verified industry participation, institutional workflows, healthcare learning, pharmacy operations, and controlled healthcare-journey architecture.
